summary: Iteration 34: Asset delete bug is FIXED. The new approach using deletedIds Set + render-time filtering works correctly. Tested single asset deletion: API call succeeds, toast shows success, first card changes from 'ENGIN1' to 'EnginQr', count decreases from 500 to 499.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/proxy/engin/delete with numeric ID returns success
Frontend: Click trash icon on first card (data-testid='asset-delete-btn-0')
Frontend: Toast shows success message 'ENGIN1 supprimé avec succès' (data-testid='delete-toast')
Frontend: First card CHANGED from 'ENGIN1' to 'EnginQr' - UI updated correctly
Frontend: Count decreased from 500 to 499 - UI updated correctly
critical_code_review_comments: The deletedIds Set approach (line 101) successfully decouples visual deletion from Redux/local state management
filteredItems (line 284-286) correctly filters out deleted items at render time
handleSingleDelete (line 195-223) properly adds deleted ID to the Set using setDeletedIds
context_for_next_testing_agent: Asset delete bug is FIXED in iteration 34. The new deletedIds Set approach works correctly. Single delete tested and verified: API succeeds, toast shows, UI updates (first card changes, count decreases). No further testing needed for this bug.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Previous approach used setAllData(prev => prev.filter(...)) which didn't trigger re-render due to dual-state pattern conflicts (local useState + Redux)
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: New approach: maintain deletedIds Set (line 101), add deleted IDs to Set after successful API call (lines 207-212), filter items at render time using filteredItems (lines 284-286)
